sae_features.label: IF coiled-coils and CHCH helices
sae_features.description: Coiled-coil/CHCH-like alpha-helical segments characteristic of cytoskeletal intermediate-filament scaffolds and mitochondrial intermembrane-space small TIM/CHCH assembly factors, often extending into adjacent low-complexity Ser/Gly-rich tracts; the feature tends to avoid cysteine-rich motifs and gives only sparse, incidental hits in unrelated enzymes.

sae_features.label: Keratin-like terminal low-complexity IDRs
sae_features.description: Intrinsically disordered, low-complexity terminal segments—especially the keratin-like head and tail regions of intermediate filament and keratin-associated proteins—enriched in Gly/Ser (often Tyr/Pro and sometimes Cys) repetitive content that serves as flexible, PTM- and crosslink-prone scaffolding; depleted in structured coiled-coil/repeat cores.

sae_features.label: Fibrous coiled-coils with disordered tails
sae_features.description: Extended, compositionally simple fibrous regions characteristic of intermediate‑filament–like scaffolds: long, low‑aromatic amphipathic coiled‑coil helices and/or flanking low‑complexity, Ser/Gly/Thr (or GAR)–rich intrinsically disordered tails, especially prevalent in cytoskeletal and axonemal proteins.

sae_features.label: Long glycine/charged low-complexity segments
sae_features.description: Long, compositionally biased stretches enriched for glycine and/or charged residues (Arg/Lys and Asp/Glu), frequently containing simple repeats such as RGG/RG and Gly–Pro blocks; these segments act as extended interaction modules, manifesting either as intrinsically disordered low‑complexity regions (common in nuclear/RNA-associated proteins) or as repetitive charged heptads in coiled‑coil rods (intermediate filaments), and they also include analogous acidic Gly‑rich stretches within catalytic regions of metabolic enzymes.

sae_features.label: N-terminal low-complexity and first helix
sae_features.description: N-terminal low-complexity tails and the immediately adjacent first alpha-helix of the next domain/coiled-coil. The feature prefers long, non-globular, Ser/Gly/Pro- and charge-rich N-terminal stretches (often harboring phosphosites) and frequently extends into the very first α-helix of BAR/ENTH/coiled-coil folds; structured core domains are largely inactive.

sae_features.label: Heptad-repeat coiled-coil scaffolds
sae_features.description: Heptad-repeat coiled-coil alpha-helices that serve as dimer/oligomer scaffolding interfaces in eukaryotic cytoskeletal, ciliary/flagellar, membrane-trafficking, and plant structural proteins.

sae_features.label: Extended coiled-coil rods
sae_features.description: Long, heptad‑repeat alpha‑helical coiled‑coil rods/stalks that form the central scaffolding/linker segments of large eukaryotic proteins; the feature recognizes coiled‑coil architecture (extended parallel helices) rather than generic helices or disordered regions.

sae_features.label: Charged coiled-coil rod cores
sae_features.description: Charged heptad‑repeat coiled‑coil “rod” segments that mediate homo-/hetero‑oligomerization in large eukaryotic scaffolding proteins. The feature preferentially recognizes the central, elongated coiled‑coil cores enriched in E/K/Q/L and ignores adjacent globular domains, transmembrane helices, and low‑complexity tails.
